name: block-push-to-main.js
purpose: Blocks git push commands targeting main or master branches; exits 2 to abort, 0 to allow.
notes: No scripts/lib helpers used — correct since no file I/O or error logging to disk. Fail-open on parse errors and large payloads. CRITICAL_HOOKS list in settings-guardian.js includes this file. Session #197 origin.

name: check-mcp-servers.js
purpose: Reads .mcp.json at SessionStart, prints available MCP server names to stdout; never exposes URLs, tokens, or secrets — only server names.
notes: Implements its own inline path-traversal guard (/^\.\.(?:[\/]|$)/.test(rel)) and output sanitizer — does NOT use scripts/lib helpers. Scope is 'project' because server names are project-specific; the portability logic is fully generic. Server count capped at 50, output at 500 chars to prevent DoS.

name: check-remote-session-context.js
purpose: SessionStart hook that fetches remote claude/* branches and warns when a newer SESSION_CONTEXT.md (higher session counter) exists on a branch other than current; helps prevent cross-locale session drift.
notes: Spawns execFileSync for append-hook-warning.js (synchronous, not async). Fetch TTL cache written to .claude/hooks/.fetch-cache.json (5-min TTL). Reads remote branches sorted by committerdate; checks up to 10 recent claude/* branches. Inline fallbacks for both lib deps. BRANCH_PREFIX ('claude/') and CONTEXT_FILE ('SESSION_CONTEXT.md') are hardcoded — must be sanitized for target project. Session #101.

name: commit-tracker.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Bash) that detects new git commits via HEAD comparison, appends structured metadata to .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl (Layer A compaction resilience), logs commit failures to commit-failures.jsonl, surfaces pre-commit hook output from .git/hook-output.log, and spawns mid-session health alerts asynchronously.
notes: ASYNC SPAWN: execFile() used in runMidSessionAlerts() — fire-and-forget with 5s timeout. If mid-session-alerts.js is absent the spawn is silently skipped. Fast bail on non-commit commands (~1ms). commit-failure-reporter.js logic merged inline. Reads .git/hook-output.log (capped 256KB, <60s old). Rotate: commit-log capped 500/300 entries, commit-failures capped 50 entries. SESSION_CONTEXT.md session counter dependency is gracefully handled when absent. wired via if-condition: Bash(git commit *)|Bash(git cherry-pick *)|Bash(git merge *)|Bash(git revert *). Session #138.

name: compact-restore.js
purpose: SessionStart hook (compact matcher) that reads .claude/state/handoff.json written by pre-compaction-save.js and injects structured recovery context (tasks, commits, git status, agents, plan, notes, audits) into Claude's context window after compaction.
notes: Only fires when matcher='compact'. Stale handoff >60 min is silently skipped. Uses inline fallbacks for both lib deps. Does NOT use safe-fs for its read — uses raw fs.readFileSync (minor gap vs CLAUDE.md §2). Output to stdout for Claude context injection; stderr for user visibility. Session #138.

name: decision-save-prompt.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (AskUserQuestion) that fires when 3+ questions or 3+ total options with a significant keyword are detected; outputs a decision-documentation reminder to stderr and persists a warning via append-hook-warning.js.
notes: Uses execFileSync to call scripts/append-hook-warning.js (synchronous child process — not async_spawn). SIGNIFICANT_KEYWORDS list is project-tunable but the docs/SESSION_DECISIONS.md reference is SoNash-specific. Always exits 0 — never blocks. Reference to 'CLAUDE.md Section 7' is project-specific and should be sanitized. From HOOKIFY_STRATEGY.md #7.

name: deploy-safeguard.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate (Bash) for firebase deploy commands; validates build freshness (.next/ newer than source), environment variables (.env.local required vars), and last test run status (test-runs.jsonl); blocks on hard failures, warns on soft issues.
notes: Hard-coded to Firebase deploy pattern and Next.js (.next/ build dir). REQUIRED_ENV_VARS (['NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_API_KEY','NEXT_PUBLIC_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID']) and SOURCE_DIRS (['app','lib','components']) are SoNash-specific. Not portable as-is; requires full replacement of deploy target, source dirs, and env vars for any non-Firebase/Next.js project. SKIP_GATES=1 kill switch present. Per D15/D20/D13 internal design refs.
if_condition: Bash(firebase deploy *)|Bash(npx firebase deploy *)|Bash(npm run deploy *)

name: ensure-fnm.sh
purpose: Bash launcher that resolves the correct Node.js binary via fnm (or PATH fallback) against the .nvmrc-pinned version and execs the given command; used as command prefix in all settings.json hook entries.
notes: Shell script (bash), not a Node.js hook. Supports exact semver, MAJOR.MINOR, and major-only .nvmrc pin formats. Validates fnm env output for shell injection (backtick, $(), semicolon guards) before eval. Cross-platform: macOS/Linux/Windows-compatible. Replaces old run-node.sh launcher (which was used in Piece 1a's JASON-OS version). Not wired as a hook event itself — it is the command prefix in every settings.json hook entry. Exits 1 if fnm unavailable, 2 if no command provided.
event: (launcher — command prefix in all settings.json hook entries, not itself an event hook)

name: firestore-rules-guard.js
purpose: PreToolUse gate (Write/Edit) that blocks removal of 'allow create, update: if false' write-block patterns from firestore.rules on protected collections (journal, daily_logs, inventoryEntries); enforces Security Rule #1 that all writes go through Cloud Functions.
notes: PROTECTED_COLLECTIONS (['journal','daily_logs','inventoryEntries']) and Firestore match path structure (/users/{userId}/...) are SoNash data-model-specific — entirely non-portable as-is. The guard logic pattern (parse rule content, check write-block expression) is conceptually portable but must be re-authored for any non-Firestore project. Two kill switches: SKIP_GATES=1 AND ALLOW_RULES_EDIT=1. Handles both Write (full content check) and Edit (old_string/new_string diff check) operations. Per D10 internal ref.

name: governance-logger.js
purpose: PostToolUse hook (Write/Edit) that logs changes to CLAUDE.md and .claude/settings.json with git diff output; appends structured JSONL to .claude/state/governance-changes.jsonl and a summary warning to hook-warnings-log.jsonl; always exits 0.
notes: Uses execFileSync to call scripts/append-hook-warning.js (synchronous, not async_spawn). GOVERNANCE_FILES is ['CLAUDE.md','.claude/settings.json'] — extensible list, rename if target project uses different governance file names. Rotate: governance-changes.jsonl capped 200/100 entries. Reads SESSION_CONTEXT.md for session counter (graceful null on absence). Per D9/D11 internal refs. Session #244.

name: backup-dir
purpose: Empty backup directory — no files present. Reserved for hook version archival.
notes: Directory exists (created 2026-04-05) but contains zero files. No archived hook versions, test harnesses, or deprecated scripts found. The directory was likely created as a placeholder for future use or was cleared at some point. D3b should confirm no hidden files were missed during their scan of hooks/lib/. No JSONL records to emit for individual backup files — directory is empty.
